thesis: Ainary can establish market dominance in AI calibration infrastructure by exploiting the 12-24 month regulatory vacuum before EU standards crystallize, creating defensible IP in multi-step agent calibration while shaping how 'accuracy' gets technically defined.

layers.name: Technical Gap Analysis
layers.description: Identify unsolved calibration problems that represent IP opportunities

layers.name: Market Readiness Assessment
layers.description: Evaluate sector-specific compliance pressure and calibration maturity

layers.name: Competitive Positioning
layers.description: Map competitor offerings and strategic gaps

layers.name: Regulatory Influence Strategy
layers.description: Develop pathway to shape CEN/CENELEC standards

sections.guidance: Present research confidence metrics, key uncertainties about multi-agent calibration composability, risks of RLHF damage permanence, and gaps in competitor positioning data

key_points: 12-24 month window before EU standards crystallize creates first-mover advantage
Multi-step agent calibration is unsolved with no incumbent owner
Healthcare/finance sectors face immediate compliance pressure with low readiness
Calibration infrastructure can be implemented at <$0.05/decision
Strategic recommendation: Build multi-agent calibration IP + shape standards
sections.guidance: Synthesize findings into actionable intelligence for C-suite decision-making
Explain the 4-layer framework for analyzing regulatory window exploitation opportunity
Present evidence-backed findings on technical gaps, market readiness, and regulatory opportunities

1) Develop IP for multi-agent calibration without logit access, 2) Target healthcare/finance sectors first, 3) Join CEN/CENELEC committees immediately, 4) Build auxiliary calibration models as competitive moat
Address uncertainty in multi-agent composability, RLHF damage permanence, competitor positioning gaps, and regulatory timeline risks

claim_ledger.text: Current calibration methods require logit access and don't compose for multi-agent systems
claim_ledger.label: E

claim_ledger.text: RLHF can permanently damage some models' calibration while others are recoverable
claim_ledger.label: E

claim_ledger.text: EU AI Act requires 'accuracy' but never mentions 'calibration', creating regulatory vacuum
claim_ledger.label: E

claim_ledger.text: Healthcare sector shows 27.3% ECE with consistency calibration vs 42% verbalized
claim_ledger.label: E
